text: vv. 19 sqq.: In Prov. 17.18, and elsewhere a warning is given against the folly of becoming surety for a friend, and verses 26, 27 of the present chapter seem to imply the same moral. These warnings perhaps refer to rash commercial speculations; where it is a question of charity, we may have the duty of making ourselves responsible on behalf of some poor man, at the risk of his defrauding us.
